AnthropogenicGeomorphologicFeature.description: An anthropogenic geomorphologic feature is a geomorphologic feature (i.e., landform) which has been created by human activity. For example, a dredged channel, midden, open pit or reclaimed land.

anthropogenicGeomorphologicFeatureType.description: The anthropogenicGeomorphologicFeatureType: AnthropogenicGeomorphologicFeatureTypeTerm is a reference from a controlled vocabulary describing the type of geomorphologic feature.

AnthropogenicGeomorphologicFeatureTypeTe.description: Refers to a vocabulary of terms describing the type of anthropogenic geomorphologic feature

GeomorphologicFeature.description: A geomorphologic feature is a kind of GeologicFeature describing the shape and nature of the Earth's land surface. These landforms may be created by natural Earth processes (e.g., river channel, beach, moraine or mountain) or through human (anthropogenic) activity (e.g., dredged channel, reclaimed land, mine waste dumps). In GeoSciML, the geomorphologic feature is modelled as a feature related (through unitDescription property) to a GeologicUnit that composes the form.

allOf.$comment: cross-BB reference to GeologicFeature in package GeologyBasic

unitDescription.description: The unitDescription property is an association that links the geomorphologic feature to a geologic description (e.g., related stratigraphic units and earth materials).

gmFeatureDescription.description: The property gmFeatureDescription:GeomorphologicUnitAbstractDescription provides a detailed morphologic description. This is a stub property in GeoSciML Basic since GeomorphologicUnitAbstractDescription is an abstract class with no concrete subtype in GeoSciML Basic.

GeomorphologicUnitAbstractDescription.description: Detailed geomorphologic unit description placeholder (stub class) for GeomorphologicUnit  Constraint: Only one instance of each subtype is allowed

NaturalGeomorphologicFeature.description: A natural geomorphologic feature is a geomorphologic feature (i.e., landform) that has been created by natural Earth processes. For example, river channel, beach ridge, caldera, canyon, moraine or mud flat.

naturalGeomorphologicFeatureType.description: The property naturalGeomorphologicFeatureType: NaturalGeomorphologicFeatureTypeTerm is a reference from a controlled vocabulary describing the type of geomorphologic feature.

activity.description: The activity property (SWE::Category) contains a category term from a controlled vocabulary describing the current activity status of the geomorphologic feature (e.g., currently active, dormant, inactive, reactivated, etc.).

NaturalGeomorphologicFeatureTypeTerm.description: Refers to a vocabulary of terms describing the type of natural geomorphologic feature

href.description: Supplies the URI to a remote resource (or resource fragment).

rel.description: The type or semantics of the relation.

type.description: A hint indicating what the media type of the result of dereferencing the link should be.

hreflang.description: A hint indicating what the language of the result of dereferencing the link should be.

title.description: Used to label the destination of a link such that it can be used as a human-readable identifier.
